titles.title: Felino: Extension of an open-source phase-field framework to geomaterial fracture

institution.institution_name: Georgia Institute of Technology, United States

citation.article_title: MOOSE: Enabling massively parallel multiphysics simulation
citation.author: Permann
citation.unstructured_citation: Permann, C. J., Gaston, D. R., Andrs, D., Carlsen, R. W., Miller, J. M., & others. (2020). MOOSE: Enabling massively parallel multiphysics simulation. SoftwareX, 11, 100430. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.softx.2020.100430

citation.article_title: libMesh: A C++ library for parallel adaptive mesh refinement/coarsening simulations
citation.author: Kirk
citation.unstructured_citation: Kirk, B. S., Peterson, J. W., Stogner, R. H., & Carey, G. F. (2006). libMesh: A C++ library for parallel adaptive mesh refinement/coarsening simulations. Engineering with Computers, 22, 237–254. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00366-006-0049-3

citation.article_title: Felino: A Modular Open-Access Software for High-Cycle Fatigue Simulation via Phase-Field Methods
citation.author: Chou
citation.unstructured_citation: Chou, D. T., & Darabi, R. (2025). Felino: A Modular Open-Access Software for High-Cycle Fatigue Simulation via Phase-Field Methods. Available at SSRN 5291375. https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.5281200

citation.article_title: Emergence of tension–compression asymmetry from a complete phase-field approach to brittle fracture
citation.author: Liu
citation.journal_title: International Journal of Solids and Structures
citation.unstructured_citation: Liu, C., & Kumar, A. (2025). Emergence of tension–compression asymmetry from a complete phase-field approach to brittle fracture. International Journal of Solids and Structures, 309, 113170. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijsolstr.2024.113170

citation.article_title: A general framework for decomposing the phase field fracture driving force, particularised to a Drucker–Prager failure surface
citation.author: Navidtehrani
citation.unstructured_citation: Navidtehrani, Y., Betegon, C., & Martinez-Paneda, E. (2022). A general framework for decomposing the phase field fracture driving force, particularised to a Drucker–Prager failure surface. Theoretical and Applied Fracture Mechanics, 121, 103555. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tafmec.2022.103555

citation.article_title: The concept of representative crack elements for phase-field fracture: Anisotropic elasticity and thermo-elasticity
citation.author: Storm
citation.journal_title: International Journal for Numerical Methods in Engineering
citation.unstructured_citation: Storm, J., Supriatna, D., & Kaliske, M. (2020). The concept of representative crack elements for phase-field fracture: Anisotropic elasticity and thermo-elasticity. International Journal for Numerical Methods in Engineering, 121(5), 779–805. https://doi.org/10.1002/nme.6244

citation.article_title: An open-source Abaqus implementation of the phase-field method to study the effect of plasticity on the instantaneous fracture toughness in dynamic crack propagation
citation.author: Molnár
citation.journal_title: Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering
citation.unstructured_citation: Molnár, G., Gravouil, A., Seghir, R., & Réthoré, J. (2020). An open-source Abaqus implementation of the phase-field method to study the effect of plasticity on the instantaneous fracture toughness in dynamic crack propagation. Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering, 365, 113004. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cma.2020.113004

citation.article_title: 2D and 3D Abaqus implementation of a robust staggered phase-field solution for modeling brittle fracture
citation.author: Molnár
citation.journal_title: Finite Elements in Analysis and Design
citation.unstructured_citation: Molnár, G., & Gravouil, A. (2017). 2D and 3D Abaqus implementation of a robust staggered phase-field solution for modeling brittle fracture. Finite Elements in Analysis and Design, 130, 27–38. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.finel.2017.03.002

citation.article_title: A review on phase field models for fracture and fatigue
citation.author: Li
citation.unstructured_citation: Li, P., Li, W., Li, B., Yang, S., Shen, Y., Wang, Q., & Zhou, K. (2023). A review on phase field models for fracture and fatigue. Engineering Fracture Mechanics, 289, 109419.

citation.article_title: Overview of phase-field models for fatigue fracture in a unified framework
citation.author: Kalina
citation.unstructured_citation: Kalina, M., Schneider, T., Brummund, J., & Kästner, M. (2023). Overview of phase-field models for fatigue fracture in a unified framework. Engineering Fracture Mechanics, 288, 109318. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.engfracmech.2023.109318

citation.author: Smith
citation.unstructured_citation: Smith, M. (2009). ABAQUS/Standard user’s manual, version 6.9. Dassault Systèmes Simulia Corp.

citation.article_title: DOLFINx: the next generation FEniCS problem solving environment
citation.author: Baratta
citation.unstructured_citation: Baratta, I. A., Dean, J. P., Dokken, J. S., Habera, M., Hale, J. S., Richardson, C. N., Rognes, M. E., Scroggs, M. W., Sime, N., & Wells, G. N. (2023). DOLFINx: the next generation FEniCS problem solving environment. preprint. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.10447666

citation.article_title: Construction of arbitrary order finite element degree-of-freedom maps on polygonal and polyhedral cell meshes
citation.author: Scroggs
citation.unstructured_citation: Scroggs, M. W., Dokken, J. S., Richardson, C. N., & Wells, G. N. (2022). Construction of arbitrary order finite element degree-of-freedom maps on polygonal and polyhedral cell meshes. ACM Transactions on Mathematical Software, 48(2), 18:1–18:23. https://doi.org/10.1145/3524456

citation.article_title: Basix: a runtime finite element basis evaluation library
citation.author: Scroggs
citation.unstructured_citation: Scroggs, M. W., Baratta, I. A., Richardson, C. N., & Wells, G. N. (2022). Basix: a runtime finite element basis evaluation library. Journal of Open Source Software, 7(73), 3982. https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.03982
